hints.text: **Concrete roads** extremely common

shared_with.note: Thailand also has many concrete roads.
shared_with.diff: Thailand drives **left** + Thai script; Philippines drives right with English signage.

hints.text: Drives on the **right** (unlike Indonesia/Malaysia/Thailand)
Plates **white with black text**; many older 2002-series plates with a **green tint**
**Octagonal metal poles** common; often a **single insulator atop a vertical side bar**

shared_with.note: Some parts of Malaysia have them.

hints.text: Middle lines **yellow or white — often combined yellow+white** (no other East-Asian country does this); yellow lines have a distinct **orange tint**
hints.uniq_note: The combined yellow+white middle line is not common in any other East/Southeast-Asian country — outside the region, always confirm with other clues.

hints.text: Tall **square yellow km stones**
Chevrons **red on white**; signposts often painted **red, orange or yellow**
**Tuk-tuks/tricycles everywhere** — often more common than cars
**English on signs everywhere** — the only major SE-Asian country where that's the norm (Filipino official)
Gen3: **white car with visible black roof**; sometimes a silvery grey car

shared_with.note: The silvery grey car also appears in Bhutan.

hints.text: **Grain spread out on roads** to dry

shared_with.note: Road-drying grain is common in Vietnam too.

hints.text: Black/white **pole boxes** with 3-letter **municipality abbreviations** (all-3 = municipality, or 2 municipality + 1 province; e.g. GMI/GMU = Gamu, Isabela) — not standardised, variants exist
**Tuk-tuk/tricycle designs vary by region** (infographic by AeroGelo)
**Province names** on many signs — but not shown on the map → memorise them (Davao de Oro = ex-Compostela Valley); landlines start 0 + area code (mobiles 09)
**Motorcycle plates colour-coded by region** — north: coloured strip on TOP, south: on the BOTTOM
Forested mountains with **dry mountainsides + pines** (rarely similar on high Mindanao roads); **Mountain-Dew-bottle decorations** common; **kuliglig tractors** around Cauayan
**Crop metas**: corn → N-Luzon + Mindanao; oil palms → Mindanao; sugarcane → Negros & Panay; pineapple → Mindanao; banana plantations → central/southern Mindanao (wild bananas grow everywhere!)

**Moluccan albizia** trees (light trunks, pinnate crowns) — esp. east & central Mindanao; western region is **Muslim-majority** (rest of PH strongly Christian); square **mesh poles** on the Zamboanga peninsula
**Amakan (woven bamboo) walls**: diamond pattern → southern half; diagonal weave ('Sawali') → Palawan + northern half
**Yellow-black striped concrete guardrails**
White guardrails with **three recurring black stripes**
**Chile-style indented square poles** → north of Manila + around Bacolod (Negros)
**Ladder poletop** (two L-profile bars + many thin connecting wires) — Manila & surroundings; countrywide lookalikes use square bars and ≤2 wires
hints.uniq_note: Similar poletops with SQUARE bars appear countrywide — but they almost never have more than two connecting wires; the true many-wired ladder is Manila-area only.

hints.text: **Chocolate Hills**: round grassy hills w/ dark brown soil (central Bohol); NW-Bohol has a Street View car **with tape on the back**
**Volcano spotlights**: Arayat — alone in flat farmland; Mayon — near-perfect cone (often cloud-capped), one of the tallest; just south of Mayon: vehicle with **thick black bars**
Destroyed houses & ruins from the **2017 Marawi siege**
Decorated white **Bataan Death March memorial waystones** (Mariveles/Bagac → San Fernando)
Trans-Palawan highway south of Puerto Princesa: **very wide (often 6 lanes) but empty**; look for the **double electrical wire**
Lamp posts **yellow with black-striped bottoms**
Images & statues of **ancient Roman soldiers**
**Rare cars**: black car w/ roof rack → General Santos; orange car w/ visible side mirrors → Corregidor Island
